title: Bare local node server script resolves SAFE (documented under-call, RC-OBS-02)

origin.description: Shape-preserving redaction of a real committed claude_desktop_config.json from the public repo JacquesGariepy/game-assistant-mcp (RC-B06, 0.3.0-rc.0 window). The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained: a single server launched via a recognized local 'node <path>/server.js' command with no env and no args beyond the script path. The identifying Windows username was neutralized.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim

humanExpectation.rationale: A bare local 'node <script>.js' command with a recognized runtime and no broad path, secret, remote, or supply-chain surface resolves to SAFE under current evidence: the source is observable (the command string is right there) and no blocking finding fires. This case pins that baseline so a future detector change is forced to update it deliberately.
knownLimitations: RC-OBS-02: an unrecognized-but-observable local executable (a node script that runs arbitrary local code) is treated as SAFE because its source is visible, not unknown. Whether such a local entrypoint should instead be REVIEW ('runs arbitrary local code, source not independently verifiable') is an open detector-calibration question tracked for an ADR; it is NOT a dangerous false-SAFE by the resolver definition (the source is not hidden). thisCaseMustNeverBeSafe is therefore left false: this case records the current contract, it does not assert SAFE is the final answer.
